texto: Having learned to write news, I now distrust newspapers as a source of information, and I am often surprised by historians who take them as primary source for knowing what really happened. I think newspapers should be read for information about how contemporaries construed events, rather than for reliable knowledge of events themselves.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Robert Darnton sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Having learned to write news, I now distrust newspapers as a source of information, and I am ofte…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, history, information, útil em momentos de information e newspapers.

texto: It simply is not true that everything is now on the Internet, but it is true that the digital resources available through the Internet have enormous potential for education and even for self-empowerment of individuals.

texto: As a graduate student at Oxford in 1963, I began writing about books in revolutionary France, helping to found the discipline of book history. I was in my academic corner writing about Enlightenment ideals when the Internet exploded the world of academic communication in the 1990s.

texto: People sometimes announce that we have entered 'the information age' as if information did not exist in other times. I think that every age was an age of information, each in its own way and according to the available media.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Darnton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “People sometimes announce that we have entered 'the information age' as if information did not ex…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, media, information pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de information e people.
